item.title: CPR\, First Aid & HIV Certification
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Phinney Center Campus\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Learn first aid\, infant\, child and adult CPR\, HIV and bloodborne pathogens\, with a focus on injury prevention and safety. Meets L/I WISHA-WAC safety standards. Certificates issued at end of class. Can you provide first aid in emergencies\, locate and take care of injuries\, help a choking infant or child and stop cardiac arrest? Learn first aid\, infant\, child and adult CPR\, HIV and bloodborne pathogens\, with a focus on injury prevention and safety. Modified to meet COVID-19 safety requirements (hands-on only\, no mouth-to mouth). Meets L/I WISHA-WAC safety standards. Certificates issued at end of class. Taught by Ken Norris\, Retired Firefighter. Location: Upper Blue Building\, Room 7.

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

item.description:

Did you know that there is a woodshop in your area? Whether you’re a newcomer to woodworking or an experienced woodworker\, the PNA woodshop is the ideal place to bring your creative projects to life. Whether you’re interested in crafting a bench\, flower bed\, jewelry box\, chair\, or cupboard\, the possibilities are endless. All you need to do is become a PNA member\, attend an orientation\, and complete two safety classes. Once you’ve done that\, you can reserve your shop time and get started on your project.

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

Event details

item.title: The Canote Brothers in Concert
item.description:
Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Phinney Center Campus: Brick (lower) Building\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, 98103\, United States

Jere and Greg Canote are identical twins from Seattle\, WA who perform as the Canote Brothers. They are known nationally through their touring and teaching at music camps and festivals across the country. The brothers have played an important role in the old-time music scene on the West Coast through a string band class they have been teaching for several decades\, and they are known for their infectious\, positive energy\, and for humorously capitalizing on their relationship as identical twins. \nThey specialize in old time fiddle tunes\, saying “We love the hypnotic ‘in the moment’ experience of playing this American Old Time music.” From the synopsis of the recent documentary about them: “Anyone close enough to be caught up in this vortex of joy\, is likely to end up feeling happier and more optimistic about themselves and the world!” \nDoors open at 7 pm for the 7:30 pm concert in the Phinney Center’s Community Hall (lower Brick Building). \nTickets available online or at prior Seattle Folklore Society concerts\, or at the door (subject to availability).

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

item.title: Homebuyer Workshops
item.description:
Monday, April 13, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Phinney Center Campus: Blue (upper) Building\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Join us to learn the steps to home ownership and the resources that help pave the way! During part one\, we’ll cover the home buying process and best practices using recently sold homes as examples. Plus\, an informative Q&A with a home inspector. During part two\, we’ll dive deeper into available downpayment assistance (DPA) programs. You’re welcome to attend part one or both sessions. \nWe meet at 6pm in the Phinney Center\, Blue Building. Snacks and NA drinks provided. Please RSVP at caitlin.lakere.com.

item.title: PETS: Park Orchard Elementary
item.description:
Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 2:15 PM

Location: Taproot Theatre, 204 N 85th St, Seattle, WA 98103

Written By Melanie Godsey For grades K–5 | 1-hour presentation including performance and actor-led talkback It’s a tail-wagging time at Pet School, where Biscuit the pup and all their furry and feathered friends learn to sit, stay, and help a human in need. When things get ruff for Biscuit’s best human friend, Corey, it’s time to fetch help. A once-close friendship has gone to the dogs, and the bus stop becomes a bullying zone. Using every paws-itively clever trick from Pet School, Biscuit will stop at nothing to help Corey and teach the other kids that being an upstander is the best trick of all. Education Goals: Bullying prevention Turning bystanders into upstanders Overcoming failure Teamwork

From Taproot Theatre

item.title: Blood Drive: Bloodworks Northwest
item.description:
Friday, April 17, 2026 at 10:15 AM

Location: Phinney Center Campus: Blue (upper) Building\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Phinney Center\, Blue Building – Room 6 Donate blood to help local transplant patients. Blood donors play a critical role in supporting local transplant patients. Over 49\,000 transplants were performed in the U.S. last year. An organ transplant may need up to 40 red cell units\, 30 platelet units\, and 25 plasma units. As a blood donor\, you make organ transplantation possible and provide patients with another renewed chance at a healthy life. Donating blood takes about an hour and is urgently needed. Book an appointment to give blood today and give hope to our community. Appointments are available between 10:15 AM to 4:15 PM. Sign up for your appointment by clicking here. Appointments are required for the best possible donation experience. Photo ID required. For safety\, we kindly request no visitors under 16 at our donation sites. Eat a hearty meal and drink plenty of fluids prior to donation.

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

Remembering his generation-defining songs and lasting impact on today’s music, this exuberant Tony Award-winning tribute to Black artistry sings, laughs, hopes, and lives out loud. Black Greek Letter Organization (BGLO) Day

From Taproot Theatre

item.title: PETS: Vinland Elementary
item.description:
Monday, April 27, 2026 at 1:15 PM

Location: Taproot Theatre, 204 N 85th St, Seattle, WA 98103

Written By Melanie Godsey For grades K–5 | 1-hour presentation including performance and actor-led talkback It’s a tail-wagging time at Pet School, where Biscuit the pup and all their furry and feathered friends learn to sit, stay, and help a human in need. When things get ruff for Biscuit’s best human friend, Corey, it’s time to fetch help. A once-close friendship has gone to the dogs, and the bus stop becomes a bullying zone. Using every paws-itively clever trick from Pet School, Biscuit will stop at nothing to help Corey and teach the other kids that being an upstander is the best trick of all. Education Goals: Bullying prevention Turning bystanders into upstanders Overcoming failure Teamwork

From Taproot Theatre

Event details

item.title: Barefoot in the Park
item.description:
Wednesday, May 13, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Taproot Theatre, 204 N 85th St, Seattle, WA 98103

By Neil Simon They’ve just said, “I do,” but can their love handle five flights of stairs and a tiny New York apartment? A buttoned-up young lawyer and his free-spirited bride stumble into married life, eccentric neighbors, leaky ceilings, and romantic chaos. Full of wit, chemistry, and charm, this classic rom-com bubbles over with laughter and warmth. Love is messy—and that's what makes it fun.

From Taproot Theatre

Event details

item.title: Conscience
item.description:
Wednesday, September 16, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Taproot Theatre, 204 N 85th St, Seattle, WA 98103

By Joe DiPietro A regional premiere based on a true story that shaped history. Margaret Chase Smith stood alone on the Senate floor, summoning the courage to speak the truth. As McCarthyism gripped the nation, she issued her Declaration of Conscience—defying fear, rejecting silence, and holding her party publicly accountable. Though her stand came at a personal cost, her voice changed history forever. This powerful and quietly electrifying story still resonates today.

From Taproot Theatre
